trigger.reason: Anthropic released Fable, a new model with cybersecurity-focused capabilities, which meets criteria 1+2 and would likely trigger sympathy-fade selling in security software and adjacent sectors.

primary_event.headline: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
primary_event.summary: Anthropic announced Fable, a new AI model designed for cybersecurity work. However, cybersecurity researchers are criticizing the model's guardrails as too restrictive for practical security research and testing applications.

secondary_events.headline: Google cuts AI subscription pricing in subscription tier wars
Decart launches Oasis 3 photorealistic world model for autonomous vehicle simulation

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Anthropic's Fable cybersecurity-focused model release, with researchers criticizing its guardrails — flagged as a sympathy-fade risk to cybersecurity/security-adjacent software.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is a cybersecurity-focused Anthropic model release, but the candidate basket is dominated by healthcare analytics, edtech, industrial machine vision, edge-AI semis, and photomask names — none of which sit in the cyber blast radius. The selloffs in HCAT, OMCL, EVH, AMBA, CGNX, and PLAB look like thematic AI/tech-sympathy fade, not a justified read of Fable's competitive substance. DUOL was up so is not a buy candidate.

discoveries.name: Omnicell, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI as both a competitive risk and an opportunity Omnicell itself is incorporating, but the core business is described as physical 'medication management solutions' and 'medication packaging systems' — pharmacy robotics and dispensing automation, not software a chatbot can replace. No mention of cybersecurity-AI competitive exposure.
discoveries.thesis: An Anthropic cybersecurity model has zero overlap with hospital pharmacy automation. Omnicell sells DEA-regulated physical dispensing cabinets and consumable blister packs that require a BAA-bound vendor and physical install — Fable doesn't touch that TAM. The curation false-victim pattern (physical robotics, Schedule II compliance) is corroborated by the filings.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stabilization/reversal within 1–3 sessions absent any company-specific 8-K; no AI-cyber language added in subsequent filings; continued capex/install activity by hospital customers.
discoveries.what_kills: An actual customer-concentration shift in the 10-Q, a missed earnings print, or a debt/covenant 8-K — the K already flags substantial debt and SaaS-renewal risk, so a fundamental miss would invalidate the sympathy-fade thesis.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Healthcare workflow hardware names get dragged into every generic AI-disruption tape even when the trigger is sector-specific (cyber). Track these for repeatable fade-and-revert patterns when AI lab news has no plausible healthcare nexus.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: OMCL is on the AI-adjacent healthcare-IT basket; a cyber-focused AI release triggers thematic selling across health-tech SaaS even when product overlap is zero.
research_pointers: Confirm no concurrent 8-K (officer departure, impairment, guidance cut) on 2026-06-10
Check sell-side notes for any Fable-related downgrade — unlikely, but should be ruled out
Look at OMCL's hospital install backlog disclosure vs. prior quarter as the real fundamental tell

discoveries.name: Cognex Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K acknowledges 'advancements in the availability of sophisticated AI algorithms and open-source machine vision platforms may lower barriers to enter our market' but also frames AI as a feature Cognex is embedding ('continuing to introduce products embedded with AI technology that augments rule-based machine vision'). No mention of cybersecurity-AI competitive exposure.
discoveries.thesis: Anthropic's Fable is a cybersecurity LLM — it has no plausible overlap with deterministic factory-floor machine vision. CGNX's moat is integrator networks, deterministic latency, and an installed base on production lines. The fade is purely thematic 'AI-tech selloff' contagion, compounded by an already-extended weekly drawdown.
discoveries.what_confirms: No AI-vision-specific competitor announcements in the days following; stable bookings commentary; reversion of the move within a week.
discoveries.what_kills: The 10-K does acknowledge open-source/AI vision as a real future risk, so a credible competing release (e.g. a Google/NVIDIA vision platform aimed at industrial QA) within the window would shift this from minimal to indirect. Also watch for a guidance cut from large auto/EV customer slowdown.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Industrial machine vision is a recurring sympathy-fade name on every consumer/SaaS AI headline. Worth building a track record of CGNX/KEYS reactions to LLM news to size future fade trades.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: CGNX is on the AI-adjacent industrial-tech basket; sympathy bid emerges on any 'AI commoditizes vision' meme regardless of whether the lab release is in a vision domain.
research_pointers: Auto/EV cyclical exposure — check if recent auto-production data is the real driver
Five-day drawdown is already extended; sizing should be smaller

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K Risk Factors make zero reference to AI as a competitive or substitution threat — risks are dominated by semiconductor cyclicality, customer concentration, China/tariff exposure, and capex. The business is physical photomasks required for every chip fab step, including AI accelerators.
discoveries.thesis: Photronics is structurally a beneficiary of AI capex (more chips → more masks), and Fable is a cybersecurity model with no semiconductor or capex implication. The selloff is mechanically backwards: a cyber-model release does not reduce TSMC/Samsung mask demand. Curation hypothesis is well-supported.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued strong leading-edge mask demand commentary; stable AI-accelerator customer order patterns; quick reversion of the move.
discoveries.what_kills: A genuine semis-capex guidance cut from a major foundry, or a tariff/export-control headline that hits PLAB's China operations (a real risk per the K).
discoveries.what_to_learn: Picks-and-shovels semis (photomasks, deposition, etch) get tarred with every 'AI bubble' fade, but their fundamentals correlate POSITIVELY with AI-lab activity. Classic mechanical mispricing.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PLAB is on the AI-adjacent semis basket; fade kicks in any time the broader AI complex weakens, even when the trigger is a non-semis substance.
research_pointers: Check whether broader semis index move explains all of PLAB's drop or there's idiosyncratic weakness
Watch tariff/export-control headlines — PLAB has material China revenue and that's the real risk vector

discoveries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors emphasize competition from EHR vendors (Epic, Oracle Health), Optum, IBM — not generic AI agents. The 10-Q does mention 'AI resources' as a content-accuracy risk but not as a substitution threat. Multiple restructurings (Project Nexus, August 2025, January 2025) signal company-specific stress.
discoveries.thesis: Anthropic Fable is a cybersecurity model with no overlap with HIPAA-bound provider data warehousing and EHR-integrated analytics. However, HCAT has real idiosyncratic stress (Project Nexus 9% RIF in Q2 2026, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn) that means this move could partly reflect ongoing fundamental concerns rather than pure sympathy.
discoveries.what_confirms: Move reverts within days alongside healthcare-IT peers; no incremental restructuring or churn news.
discoveries.what_kills: The 10-Q explicitly flags continuing DOS-Ignite churn and revenue decline — if a sell-side note tied today's move to fundamentals rather than the Fable headline, the 'sympathy fade' read is wrong.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Small-cap healthcare-IT names with active restructurings are bad sympathy-fade vehicles: it's hard to separate AI-thematic selling from company-specific bleed. Prefer cleaner balance sheets for this trade.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: HCAT is on the AI-adjacent healthcare-analytics basket; thematic selling hits whenever AI labs ship, regardless of cyber-specific substance.
research_pointers: Check for any incremental Project Nexus / churn commentary in the days around 2026-06-10
Five-day return is strongly positive — much of today's drop is giving back a recent pop, which weakens the sympathy-fade trade

discoveries.name: Ambarella, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Filings explicitly position Ambarella as an AI beneficiary: 'Most of our SoCs... support AI functionality' and 'computationally intense AI applications for deep fusion... VLMs, LLMs, VLAs and reasoning models in edge devices.' No mention of cybersecurity-AI as competitive risk.
discoveries.thesis: Ambarella is an edge-AI inference beneficiary, and Fable is a cloud cybersecurity LLM with no edge-inference overlap. The curation note ('direct AI beneficiary') is supported. However, AMBA already had a weak 5-day tape, so part of this is broader semis fade, not pure Fable sympathy.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable design-win commentary; reversion alongside peers; no NVIDIA/Qualcomm announcement of competing edge-AI SoC in the window.
discoveries.what_kills: The K explicitly flags NVIDIA, Qualcomm, Mobileye as competitors with greater resources in automotive AI — a competitive release from one of those (not Anthropic) would be the real threat. Also customer concentration (WT = ~70% of revenue, Insta360 large end-customer) is a real fundamental risk.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Edge-AI silicon trades like a beta-2 AI complex name; on lab-news fades it usually overshoots and reverts when the fundamental thesis (AI compute everywhere) reasserts.
discoveries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: AMBA is on the AI-adjacent edge-semis basket; the cyber-AI trigger drags semis broadly via thematic AI tape.
research_pointers: Check whether SOX/SMH was down by similar magnitude — this could be pure beta, not sympathy fade
Five-day return is sharply negative — already extended, size accordingly

skipped.reason: Risk factors reveal genuine fundamental stress (Performance Suite risk, oncology cost overruns, partner concentration, OBBBA Medicaid risk) and stock price ~$4.47 / market cap ~$500M suggests broken-name dynamics; sympathy-fade lens unreliable here.
Up +3.44% today — not a sympathy-fade BUY candidate (screen requires negative move_pct).
